free_text: Proton-Induced Reactions on Titanium with Energies
Between 13 and 45 MeV
.Institut Fuer Kernchemie
Primary Proton Energies 45 and 30 MeV.
Degradation of the Proton Energy was Calculated
According to C.F.Williamson Et Al., Cea-R3042(1966).
Due to Straggling of the Protons in the Stack the Beam
Current Could not be Measured Directly by Means of a
Faraday Cup. Therefore, Two Au-Foils (12.5 MICROMETER
EACH) Were Irradiated Under Identical Conditions, One
In Front of the Stack, the Other in Front of a Faraday
Cup. By Comparing the Activities in Both of these Foils
The Total Charge For the Stack Irradiated was Obtained.
Two Different Stacks were Used For an Energy Range
From 45 to About 27 MeV and from 30 to About 13 MeV,
Respectively. Each Stack Consisted of Foils of Natural
Titanium of High Purity, Thickness 25 Micrometer and
Of Al-Foils of Varying Thicknesses Serving As Energy
Degraders. In Front of Each Stack an Au-Foil of 12.5
Micrometer Thickness was Mounted For Beam Current
Determination.
.Resolution 2.1 KeV at 1332 KeV
Uncertainty in the Proton Energy Including
Errors in the Thickness of Target Foils and Energy
Straggling.
The Errors Given Include Errors in Current
Integration, Counting Statistics and Photopeak
Integration, Detector Efficiency, Target Tickness and
Half-Life. Not Included are the Errors in Absolute
Branching Ratios. However, Inconsistencies in the
Branching Ratios are Considered in Those Cases in Which
Several Undisturbed Gamma-Rays were Available.
Lakw
Some Corrections have Been Included by
CAJAD
Lower Case. Some Reaction Lines are
Corrected
Last checking has been done.
Below 15 MeV V-48 is Predominantly Produced Via the
Reaction Ti-48(P,N)
